content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of NNN integers and an integer BBB. Your task is to determine whether there exists a pair of indices (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) such that 1≤i<j≤N1 \leq i < j \leq N1≤i<j≤N and ∣A[i]−A[j]∣=B|A[i] - A[j]| = B∣A[i]−A[j]∣=B.

Function DeclarationFunction NamehasPairWithDifferencehasPairWithDifferencehasPairWithDifference – This function checks if there is any valid pair in the array with an absolute difference exactly equal to BBB.

Parameters
AAA : A list/array of integers of length NNN, representing the input sequence.
NNN : An integer representing the number of elements in the array.
BBB : An integer representing the target absolute difference.
Return ValueReturns an integer(or boolean): `1` (or `true`) if such a pair exists, and `0` (or `false`) otherwise.
constraints: Sum of N over all test cases ≤ 200000
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case starts with a line containing two integers N and B. The next line contains N space‑separated integers A1…AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing 1 if there exists a pair (i,j) with i<j and |Ai‑Aj|=B, otherwise output 0.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: 80‑2 = 78, so pair exists.\nTest case 2: 20‑(-10) = 30, pair exists.\nTest case 3: No two numbers differ by 5.
First test: B=0 and value 1 appears twice, so answer 1.\nSecond test: No two numbers differ by 5.
-15 and -5 differ by 10, therefore answer is 1.

solution.approach: For each test case store all numbers in an unordered hash set. Iterate through the array: for current value x check if x+B or x‑B is already in the set (or will appear later). If B==0 we need at least two equal numbers, which is naturally detected because when we see a value that is already in the set we can answer true. If any check succeeds, output 1; otherwise after processing all elements output 0. This runs in O(N) average time per test case and O(N) extra memory.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case (average), O(total N) over all test cases
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) per test case for the hash set
